questions.prompt: Why is a control link like ELRS preferred for long range?
options: Because it makes the drone lighter
Because its high receiver sensitivity, very low latency, and flexible packet rate (lower rate = longer reach) provide more reliable long range
Because it automatically increases video quality
Because it increases battery capacity
questions.explanation: ELRS offers a reliable long-range control link thanks to its high sensitivity, low latency, and adjustable packet rate (longer reach at lower packet rates).

questions.prompt: What is the key difference between an omni (omnidirectional) antenna and a directional antenna?
options: Omni is only for video, directional is only for control

Omni radiates the signal equally in all directions (flexible but limited range in any one direction); directional focuses the signal in a specific direction (much farther in that direction, weak outside it)
They are exactly the same, only the shapes differ
A directional antenna is always better in every situation
questions.explanation: Omni radiates/receives equally in all directions, is flexible, but limited in range in any one direction. A directional antenna focuses energy in one direction; range increases greatly there but the signal weakens outside it. Diversity combines the two.

questions.prompt: Why is polarization important for the VTX and goggle antennas?
options: It's not important, any combination works
They must be the same polarization (e.g. both RHCP); a mismatch (RHCP VTX + LHCP goggles) drastically lowers the signal and nearly kills the range
Using different polarization increases range
Polarization only affects the battery
questions.explanation: FPV antennas are usually circularly polarized, and the VTX and goggle antennas must be the same polarization. A mismatch seriously lowers the signal; for long range this means the range is gone.

questions.prompt: How should battery/range planning be done on a long-range flight?
options: Only the outbound distance is calculated, the return is not considered
You go as fast as possible by pinning the throttle
Accounting for the round trip (and wind), you fly at an efficient cruise speed and leave plenty of margin for the return; voltage is monitored continuously and you turn back early
You fly until the battery is completely empty
questions.explanation: On long range, an efficient cruise speed extends the range; the return and wind (a tailwind on the way out becomes a headwind on the way back) must be accounted for, and you should turn back early before the voltage runs out.

questions.prompt: Which statement is most correct about failsafe (the drone's behavior if the link is lost)?
options: It's only needed in racing and unimportant for long range
It must be set up and tested before flying; an unconfigured/wrong failsafe means the drone flies off uncontrollably when the link is lost and poses a serious hazard
Failsafe is automatically perfectly configured on every drone, no need to touch it
Failsafe is a setting that increases the drone's speed
questions.explanation: Failsafe determines what happens when the link is lost (cut the motors or return home via GPS). It's vital for long range; it must be set up and tested before flying, or the drone could fly off out of control.
